rationale: Both new doc comments accurately describe the actual code. Reviewer.model() (config.go ~139) returns Model if non-empty else Family, returning "" only when both are empty; the comment states exactly this and correctly notes the typed-string field cannot distinguish absent from explicit-empty without a *string/UnmarshalYAML rework. validateReviewerEntries (~535) only refuses when r.model()=="", i.e. the neither-set case; its comment correctly states {model:"", family:<f>} is NOT refused there. Both comments use the word 'supersedes' as required. Checked the supersession claim against spec 112's actual text: R1 (spec.md line 86) says 'an entry with family and no model is valid and resolves its model to the family string,' while R4 (line 92) lists 'a reviewer entry with neither model nor family, or an empty-string model' as refused -- the exact contradiction and exact quoted phrase the new comments cite verbatim. TestLoad_EmptyStringModel's own doc comment also correctly summarizes this. Ran the new test: both subtests pass, exercising real Load/PanelGateReviewerSlots paths.
